{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] made of [[andesite]], with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[Grave goods]] included iron products, [[Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from [[Sōjōdaigakumae Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Kamao Kofun | native_name =釜尾古墳| | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamao Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|50|20.45|N|130|41|47.89|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|50|20.45|N|130|41|47.89|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] made of [[andesite]], with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[Grave goods]] included iron products, [[Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from [[Sōjōdaigakumae Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kumamoto-guide.jp/spots/detail/126 Kumamoto-to-Heart tourist guide home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] made of [[andesite]], with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[Grave goods]] included iron products, [[Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from [[Sōjōdaigakumae Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Kamao Kofun | native_name =釜尾古墳| | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamao Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|50|20.45|N|130|41|47.89|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|50|20.45|N|130|41|47.89|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] made of [[andesite]], with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[Grave goods]] included iron products, [[Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from [[Sōjōdaigakumae Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[Grave goods]] included iron products, [[Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from [[Sōjōdaigakumae Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Kamao Kofun | native_name =釜尾古墳| | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamao Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|50|20.45|N|130|41|47.89|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|50|20.45|N|130|41|47.89|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] made of [[andesite]], with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[Grave goods]] included iron products, [[Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from [[Sōjōdaigakumae Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Kamao Kofun | native_name =釜尾古墳| | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamao Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|50|20.45|N|130|41|47.89|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|50|20.45|N|130|41|47.89|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] made of [[andesite]], with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[Grave goods]] included iron products, [[Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from [[Sōjōdaigakumae Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] made of [[andesite]], with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[Grave goods]] included iron products, [[Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from [[Sōjōdaigakumae Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Kamao Kofun | native_name =釜尾古墳| | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamao Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|50|20.45|N|130|41|47.89|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|50|20.45|N|130|41|47.89|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] made of [[andesite]], with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[Grave goods]] included iron products, [[Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from [[Sōjōdaigakumae Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] made of [[andesite]], with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[Grave goods]] included iron products, [[Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kamao Kofun]][[en:Kamao Kofun]][[ja:釜尾古墳]][[zh:釜尾古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24866261]] Wikidata name: Kamao Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24866261]] Wikidata name: Kamao Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kamao Kofun | native_name =釜尾古墳| | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamao Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.839014|N|130.696636|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|50|20.45|N|130|41|47.89|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from [[:en:Sōjōdaigakumae Station|Sōjōdaigakumae Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kumamoto-guide.jp/spots/detail/126 Kumamoto-to-Heart tourist guide home page]{{in lang|ja}}

It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from [[:en:Sōjōdaigakumae Station|Sōjōdaigakumae Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamao Kofun]][[en:Kamao Kofun]][[ja:釜尾古墳]][[zh:釜尾古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24866261]] Wikidata name: Kamao Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24866261]] Wikidata name: Kamao Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Kamao Kofun | native_name =釜尾古墳| | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamao Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.839014|N|130.696636|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|50|20.45|N|130|41|47.89|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from [[:en:Sōjōdaigakumae Station|Sōjōdaigakumae Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kumamoto-guide.jp/spots/detail/126 Kumamoto-to-Heart tourist guide home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from [[:en:Sōjōdaigakumae Station|Sōjōdaigakumae Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kumamoto-guide.jp/spots/detail/126 Kumamoto-to-Heart tourist guide home page]{{in lang|ja}}

{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from [[:en:Sōjōdaigakumae Station|Sōjōdaigakumae Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from [[:en:Sōjōdaigakumae Station|Sōjōdaigakumae Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]], with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included iron products, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from [[:en:Sōjōdaigakumae Station|Sōjōdaigakumae Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|50|20.45|N|130|41|47.89|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite}}, with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included iron products, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from {{ill|Sōjōdaigakumae Station|en|Sōjōdaigakumae Station|WD=Q5358620|lt=Sōjōdaigakumae Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|WD=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kumamoto-guide.jp/spots/detail/126 Kumamoto-to-Heart tourist guide home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus is about five minutes from {{ill|Sōjōdaigakumae Station|en|Sōjōdaigakumae Station|WD=Q5358620|lt=Sōjōdaigakumae Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite}}, with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included iron products, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from {{ill|Sōjōdaigakumae Station|en|Sōjōdaigakumae Station|WD=Q5358620|lt=Sōjōdaigakumae Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included iron products, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from {{ill|Sōjōdaigakumae Station|en|Sōjōdaigakumae Station|WD=Q5358620|lt=Sōjōdaigakumae Station|1=Sōjōdaigakumae Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}}, with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included iron products, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. {{nihongo|'''Kamao Kofun'''|釜尾古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamao neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921 with the area under protection expanded in 2022.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202996|title=釜尾古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kamao Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus, located at an elevation of about 50 meters on the Kamao Hills on the right bank of the Iseri River in the northwest part of the city. It is known to have been an ancient Kofun since at least the mid-[[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] as it appears in historical records dated 1769. When the Kamao Tenjin Shrine was constructed to the immediate west of the tumulus, the western half of the mound was destroyed. Although the mound has been deformed, it is estimated to be 18 meters in diameter and 6 meters in height. A surrounding moat with an inner diameter of 28-29 meters and a width of 3-4 meters was discovered in 1990. The interior contains a horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|qid=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}}, with a double-sided entrance gate opening to the south. The total length is about 9.6 meters, with the burial chamber mesuring 3.6 meters long, 3.6 meters wide and 3 meters high. The entrance passage is 6 meters long and one meter wide, but only 0.7 meters high. The antechamber is 1.75 by 0.9 meters, and 1.5 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]]. The side walls of the chamber are made of broken stones, the lower part of which is painted red at a height of about 1.8 meters, and the upper part painted white. The decorative designs are painted in red, blue and white on the pillars and capstone of the entrance gate that make up the front wall, with concentric circles, triangles, double-row triangles and double-legged rings. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included iron products, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery, tubular beads, swords, knives, armor and horse equipment. Based on the structure of the stone chamber and the murals, it is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about five minutes from {{ill|Sōjōdaigakumae Station|en|Sōjōdaigakumae Station|qid=Q5358620|lt=Sōjōdaigakumae Station|1=Sōjōdaigakumae Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|qid=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
